methodsAttempted.query: site:yu.edu.jo Yarmouk University artificial intelligence policy ChatGPT generative AI academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Initial English Firecrawl search returned no official policy results.
Search found a faculty workshop page and research/publication records; the workshop was rejected as event/training context rather than university policy evidence.
Arabic official-domain search found the official university announcement approving generative AI use policies and a Graduate Studies bylaws page linking the policy PDF.
Followed the Graduate Studies bylaws page link to the official AI-2024.pdf policy document.
Checked robots.txt for www.yu.edu.jo and graduatestudies.yu.edu.jo; the fetched public news, bylaws, and PDF paths were not disallowed.

artifacts.summary: Official Yarmouk discovery found a university news article announcing Board of Deans approval of generative AI use policies, the Graduate Studies bylaws page listing the policy, and the linked Arabic policy PDF. A Faculty of Educational Sciences ChatGPT workshop page was official but rejected because it is event/training coverage rather than policy or guidance authority.

expectedThemes: ethics approval for human or animal data

stopConditions: Stop after official university-level policy sources support direct claim candidates.
Do not infer policy from faculty workshop or publication pages.
Preserve Arabic source snippets as canonical evidence; English display snippets are helper translations only.

artifacts.sourceTitle: "اليرموك" تقر سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحثي
سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحث العلمي في جامعة اليرموك

artifacts.claimText: Yarmouk University's Board of Deans approved policies for using generative AI applications in academic and scientific research work.

citation.citationTitle: "اليرموك" تقر سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحثي
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Yarmouk's generative AI policy applies to all undergraduate and graduate students, faculty members, and people involved in preparing, evaluating, or supervising academic and research work.

citation.citationTitle: سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحث العلمي في جامعة اليرموك
citation.publisher: Yarmouk University Deanship of Scientific Research and Graduate Studies
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Yarmouk allows generative AI tools in academic or scientific research work across stages such as research, design, planning, drafting, analysis, writing, audiovisual content production, or editing, provided the use is announced, pre-approved, and transparent.

citation.citationTitle: سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحث العلمي في جامعة اليرموك
citation.publisher: Yarmouk University Deanship of Scientific Research and Graduate Studies
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Yarmouk's announced policy says the permitted percentage of disclosed generative AI-generated content is based on the university's academic integrity standards and does not exceed 30%, with adaptation possible for departments, faculties, and centers.

citation.citationTitle: "اليرموك" تقر سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحثي
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Yarmouk requires faculty members, researchers, and students to disclose their use of generative AI tools and applications in academic and research work clearly and transparently.

citation.citationTitle: سياسات استخدام تطبيقات الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في العمل الأكاديمي والبحث العلمي في جامعة اليرموك
citation.publisher: Yarmouk University Deanship of Scientific Research and Graduate Studies
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Yarmouk's policy says researchers must obtain approval from the university human research ethics board or animal research ethics committee when using generative AI applications in research involving generated data about human or animal subjects.

artifacts.notes: Official university news directly supports approval of generative AI use policies.
Scope claim is limited to groups and work categories named in the official policy PDF.
Allowed-use claim preserves the policy's stated conditions: announcement, prior approval, and transparency.
Claim is scoped to the announced percentage and notes that departments, faculties, and centers may adapt instructions.
Disclosure requirement is directly supported by the policy PDF and the official news announcement.
Data and ethics claim is narrowed to the policy's explicit human/animal research-data condition.

artifacts.title: Yarmouk University generative AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source evidence for Yarmouk University's Board-approved generative AI policy covering academic and research work, permitted use conditions, disclosure, a 30% announced cap for disclosed AI-generated content, and ethics approval for certain human or animal data uses.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Arabic original-language snippets are authoritative; English display snippets are helper translations only.
The PDF extraction required right-to-left text normalization, so policy claims were kept narrow and cross-checked against the official news announcement and bylaws link.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
